sanskrit:
एषा तेऽभिहिता साङ्ख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां शृणु।
बुद्ध्या युक्तो यया पार्थ कर्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि॥
transliteration:
eṣā te ’bhihitā sāṅkhye buddhir yoge tv imāṃ śṛṇu |
buddhyā yukto yayā pārtha karma-bandhaṃ prahāsyasi ||
literal_meaning: This analytical wisdom has been declared to you; now hear this (same) wisdom as applied to action. Endowed with this intelligence, O Pārtha, you will cast off the bondage of karma.
contextual_meaning: Krishna signals a pivot from pure insight to embodied practice: the same truth that looks abstract in Sāṅkhya becomes a lived discipline in Karma-yoga.  Arjuna is invited to translate realisation into action so that his paralysis (fear of karmic backlash) dissolves.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance: Arjuna intellectually grasps the eternal self yet still feels stuck in the field of action; needs a bridge between knowing and doing.
trigger_scenario: A mid-career professional understands the concept of work-life balance in theory but keeps over-working to please others; feels trapped by the very success he created.
anchor_text: Act wisely, and action itself dissolves the bondage of karma.
